Dalla's babe (Mance Rayder's son, being raised by Gilly)
The princeling whose head was dashed against the wall (unnamed)
King Daeron the First (the Young Dragon)
Bran Stark (implied — secret Sam keeps about "companions")
Dickon Tarly (implied — father could have pretended he was only son)
The oarsmaster with a Tyroshi wife (unnamed)
House Hightower (Lord Leyton, Gunthor, Baelor, Garth, Humfrey, the Mad Maid)
House Lannister (mentioned — "as wealthy as the Lannisters")
The Night's Watch (Sam is a brother; Jon is Lord Commander)
The Citadel (maesters' institution; novices, acolytes, archmaesters)
Ironmen / ironborn (raiding forces; hundreds of ships)
The Seneschal's office (rotates yearly by lot among archmaesters)
The septry (novices hurrying toward it at the Citadel)
The gods (Sam hopes the gods will be kind to both children — unspecified)
Summer Islanders (Kojja Mo, Quhuru Mo, Xhondo; feathered cloaks, goldenheart bows, love of fruit and fish, Summer Tongue)
Dornish / Dornishmen (Alleras is half-Dornish; Dornish drawl; Dornish yew bows; "quarrel like Dornishmen and Marchers")
Tyroshi (merchantmen; colored whiskers; ironmen disguised as Tyroshi; Tyrosh tongue)
Ironborn / Ironmen (raiders; longships; reavers; unprecedented fleet operations)
Marchers (quarrel with Dornishmen, per Alleras's analogy)
Wildlings (Mance Rayder's people; Gilly is a wildling girl)
Valyrians / the Freehold (sorcerers who used glass candles; sorcery rooted in blood or fire)
First Men (implied — Age of Heroes reference)
Glass candle (obsidian/dragonglass; burns in Marwyn's chamber; Valyrian magical artifact)
Goldenheart bows (Summer Islander weapons; shoot farther and truer than Dornish yew)
Maester Aemon's body (aboard the Cinnamon Wind)
Banner: red eye with black pupil beneath black iron crown supported by two crows
Huntress (war galley; figurehead of maiden in leaves with spear)
Honor of Oldtown (Lord Hightower's four-decked banner ship)
The boom across Oldtown harbor (chain linking two dozen rotted hulks)
Statue of King Daeron the First (stone horse, sword toward Dorne)
Green sphinxes at Citadel gates (lion bodies, eagle wings, serpent tails; one male face, one female)
Hand-drawn map of the Citadel (Sam buys at Scribe's Hearth)
Books of spells (Lord Leyton and the Mad Maid consulting them atop the Hightower)

mentions.raw_name: Glass candle burning (obsidian; flame does not flicker; distorts colors; enables far-seeing, dream-entering, long-distance communication)
Valyrian sorcery (rooted in blood or fire)
The prophecy of Azor Ahai / the prince that was promised ("born amidst salt and smoke, beneath a bleeding star")
Wights at the Fist of First Men (referenced in Sam's account)
The Other on his dead horse (referenced in Sam's account)
Dragons (Xhondo saw in Qarth; Daenerys's dragons)
Books of spells (Lord Leyton and the Mad Maid consulting them)
Ironborn raids on the Reach (current; unprecedented scale; hundreds of ships; sacking, raiding, infiltration attempts)
War of the Five Kings (implied — Lord Randyll fighting in the riverlands; Lord Paxter's fleet committed)
Battle in Whispering Sound (recent; corpses, wrecked ships, burned villages)
The conquest of Dorne (statue of King Daeron I with sword toward Dorne)
Fist of First Men (battle with wights, referenced)
Mutiny at Craster's Keep (murder of the Old Bear, referenced)
Lord Commander (of the Night's Watch — Jon Snow)
Seneschal (of the Citadel — rotates yearly by lot; currently Theobald acting for Walgrave)
Acolyte (Alleras; Lorcas has been one for fifty years)
Novice (Sam hopes to become one; Leo Tyrell; pink-cheeked novices; Pate described as novice)
Lord (Leyton Hightower, Paxter Redwyne, Randyll Tarly)
Dornish yew (bow material, inferior to goldenheart)
The "bitch queen in King's Landing" (Cersei Lannister, unnamed but clear from context)
"Grey sheep" (Marwyn's term for the mainstream archmaesters)
The pirate lord who once held the Ravenry in the Age of Heroes
